page: Amazon Web Services i4g.4xlarge is a storage-optimized server instance powered by the ARM64 architecture. It features 16 dedicated physical cores utilizing the AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the instance includes a bundled 3750 GB NVMe SSD. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 9 Gbps, managed via the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark data indicates average performance across multi-core operations and database tasks. The instance achieved a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 24,287.8 and a PassMark database operations score of 10,352.38. Redis SET operations reached 18.83 million operations per second, while static web serving throughput was measured at 18.67 GB/sec. However, single-core performance is a notable tradeoff, with Geekbench single-core scoring in the bottom 25% at 976.0, and single-threaded gzip compression also ranking in the lower tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the integration of custom Graviton ARM64 silicon with a high-capacity 3750 GB NVMe SSD provides a dense resource profile for storage-heavy workloads without the premium of x86 licensing. This hardware configuration is suited for transactional databases, caching layers like Redis, and web serving applications that require high local storage capacity and moderate multi-core processing. It is less suited for single-threaded tasks, cryptography-heavy applications, or large-scale LLM inference due to lower single-core and vector instruction benchmarks.
description: Amazon Web Services i4g.4xlarge is an ARM64 storage-optimized instance powered by 16 dedicated physical cores of the AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.5 GHz. It features 128.0 GB of RAM, a bundled 3750 GB NVMe SSD, and a baseline network bandwidth of 9 Gbps under the Nitro hypervisor. Benchmarks show average multi-core and database performance, with Redis SET operations reaching 18.83 million operations per second and PassMark database operations scoring 10,352.38. However, single-core performance represents a tradeoff, with Geekbench single-core and single-threaded compression benchmarks falling into the lower 25th percentile. This instance offers qualitative cost efficiency for storage-intensive deployments by combining custom ARM silicon with high-capacity local NVMe storage. It is suited for database operations, caching layers, and web serving, while being less optimal for single-threaded or compute-heavy workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i4g.4xlarge is an ARM64 storage-optimized instance featuring 16 Graviton cores, 128 GB RAM, and a 3750 GB NVMe SSD, suited for database and caching workloads.

tagline: An ARM64 storage-optimized platform combining dedicated Graviton cores with high-capacity local NVMe SSD storage for database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 16 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 ARM64 cores at 2.5 GHz
Equipped with 128 GB of system memory and a 9 Gbps baseline network
Includes 3750 GB of bundled local NVMe SSD storage
Delivers average multi-core performance and 18.83 million Redis SET operations per second
Exhibits lower single-core and single-threaded compression performance
Suited for database operations, caching layers, and web serving
